t_ch: 不好意思，我开玩笑的。
t_en: Sorry, I'm just kidding.

t_ch: …你最好是。
t_en: ...That'd better be the case.

t_ch: …你倒是说词啊！
t_en: ...Please say something!

t_ch: …你想让我说什么啊？！
t_en: ...What do you want me to say!?

t_ch: 怎么？是我，不满意？
t_en: What's the problem? Are you unhappy to see me?

t_ch: 你的语气…好欠打……
t_en: Your tone... is annoying...

t_ch: 哎，行了…我们还是聊回茉莉小姐吧。
t_en: That's it... We should just talk about Molly.

t_ch: 自从来到机械屋给希露瓦当助手之后，茉莉整个人明显精神多了。
t_en: Molly's looking more spirited ever since she started working as Serval's assistant at the workshop.

t_ch: 以前在技术部的时候，茉莉每天不是在和人吵架就是在去吵架的路上，整个人身上充满了消极的能量。
t_en: Back at the Technology Division, if Molly wasn't already in a quarrel with someone, she'd be on her way to one. She was always so on edge.

t_ch: 很高兴能看到她慢慢好起来…当然啦，这部分功劳你也要占大头哦。
t_en: I'm glad to see her slowly brighten up... Of course, much of it was thanks to you.

t_ch: 没有和公司签约，你遗憾吗？
t_en: Do you regret not signing the contract with the IPC?

t_ch: 是说之前询问我意见的那件事吗？唔...当然不会啦~
t_en: You mean the thing you asked my opinion on before? Well... of course not~

t_ch: 虽然贝洛伯格没有被公司并购或接手，但还是有不少员工留驻在了城里。
t_en: Although Belobog has not been acquired or taken on by the IPC, many of their employees have remained stationed in the city.

t_ch: 我这里的一份资料显示：自从这些黑衣人驻扎下来后，造物之柱的工程效率整体提升了近<unbreak>40</unbreak>个百分点。
t_en: There is a report here that shows productivity on the Pillars of Creation construction project has increased by nearly forty percent since these people in black got stationed here.

t_ch: 如果他们真的愿意把压箱底的技术都拿出来分享，施工速度肯定会翻倍...不，甚至翻上好几番都有可能！
t_en: If they really are willing to share their technology unreservedly with us, then production speed will definitely double... No! It could even triple or quadruple!

t_ch: 从数据上看，布洛妮娅大人做出了非常正确的选择。对贝洛伯格的大家来说，这可能就是最好的结果了吧？
t_en: Looking at the data, Lady Bronya made a very appropriate decision. This might even be the best possible result for the people of Belobog.

t_ch: 欢迎来到铁卫禁区，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Welcome to the Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我已吩咐过全军对你和同伴多加关照，但还请你务必不要做出逾矩的举动。
t_en: I've instructed the soldiers to treat you hospitably, but I do have to remind you to be considerate of your actions.

t_ch: 所谓公事公办——此类行为一经发现，我身为戍卫官也无法保你周全。
t_en: Rules are rules — once any such acts are discovered, even I, the Captain, can't guarantee your safety.

t_ch: 我会多加注意。
t_en: Duly noted.

t_ch: 不胜感激。希望你能享受此次贝洛伯格之行……
t_en: I would be deeply grateful. I hope you will enjoy this trip to Belobog...

t_ch: 或者，以你们对这个世界的称谓来说——希望你能享受此次雅利洛-Ⅵ之行。
t_en: Or, as you all call this world — I hope you will enjoy this trip to Jarilo-VI.

t_ch: 戍卫官好严格哦。
t_en: The Captain's so uptight.

t_ch: 大守护者与她的律法当如环绕这贝洛伯格的铁壁，任何事物都不能凌驾于其上。
t_en: The Supreme Guardian and her laws are akin to an iron wall around Belobog. Nothing in this place is above them.

t_ch: 身为曾经并肩作战的战友，我不希望看到你身处险境。因此，还望你对我提出的建议多加考虑。
t_en: As a comrade who once fought by your side, I do not wish to see harm befall you. Which is why I hope you will reconsider my suggestion.

t_ch: 严肃的话题就先到这里吧。我正准备稍事休息，想聊聊其他的话题吗？
t_en: But enough of this stuffy topic. I'm about to go on a break. Do you have something else you wish to talk about?

t_ch: 那就来聊聊天吧…
t_en: Let's have a chat then...

t_ch: 戍卫官都负责什么样的工作？
t_en: What are a Captain's duties?

t_ch: 你能想到的、银鬃铁卫需要承担的一切职责。
t_en: Anything you can think of, the Silvermane Guards are responsible for.

t_ch: 在这之上，戍卫官还要负责调度全体铁卫，以便每个人都能正确地行使自己的职能。
t_en: Above all else, the Captain is charged with organizing the Guards, ensuring that everyone performs their job properly.

t_ch: 我理解了。
t_en: I understand.

t_ch: 正如你所想，戍卫官的工作并不复杂。
t_en: It is as you imagined, a Captain's job isn't all that complicated.

t_ch: 我——不，我们——不过是贝洛伯格人日常生活的守护者罢了。
t_en: I— No, we are but guardians safeguarding the well-being of Belobogians.

t_ch: 好笼统的描述…
t_en: That's a broad description...

t_ch: 城市防务事关重大。其牵涉事项之多，普通居民恐怕难以想象。
t_en: City defense is an important duty. It is linked to many matters that your average citizens can't fathom.

t_ch: 但归根到底，我们不过是贝洛伯格人日常生活的守护者——你只需要了解这点就好。
t_en: But fundamentally, we are but guardians safeguarding the well-being of Belobogians — that's all you need to understand.

t_ch: 你和姐姐的关系如何？
t_en: How's your relationship with your big sister?

t_ch: 希露瓦…对于她的种种行为，我只能是睁一只眼闭一只眼了。
t_en: Serval... I can only turn a blind eye to her various actions.

t_ch: 对于朗道家而言，姐姐的行为无疑是离经叛道。也难怪我们的管家隔三差五便跑去机械屋，劝谏她找份正经工作。
t_en: To the Landaus, my big sister's acts are irrefutably traitorous to the clan. It is no wonder our butler regularly goes to the workshop to persuade her to find a proper job.

t_ch: 听说佩拉是你的副官？
t_en: I hear Pela is your aide?

t_ch: 是的，但我们的交流基本止于工作——不过她与希露瓦来往尤为密切，因此我们在工作之外的生活中也有所接触。
t_en: Yes, but our interactions mostly revolve around work. Though, she is quite close to Serval, which is to say, our lives do somewhat intersect outside of work.

t_ch: 其实，我平时都在尽可能躲着她…我总觉得她身上有种，嗯…压迫感。
t_en: In truth, I try to avoid her most of the time... I always feel that she's sort of hmm... overbearing.

t_ch: 曾经有一次，我见她工作繁忙，便提出替她处理某张报表。
t_en: There was once when I saw that she was swamped with work, so I suggested helping her finish up a report.

t_ch: 当我将报表交给她时，没曾想她竟一眼从海量的数据中挑出各种错漏，紧接着便对我开始进行条分缕析的批驳……
t_en: When I submitted it to her, I didn't think she'd be able to point out so many mistakes at a mere glance, followed by a barrage of detailed remarks...

t_ch: 虽然事后她亲自向我道歉了，我也明白这是自己工作上的疏漏，但这件事还是给我上了一课…最好不要擅自插手佩拉的工作。
t_en: She personally apologized to me after that, and I also acknowledged my tardiness, but that matter sure taught me a lesson... It's best not to get involved in Pela's work.

t_ch: 暂时想不到该聊些什么了。
t_en: I can't think of what to talk about right now.

t_ch: 无妨，想到哪里就聊到哪里，不必感到拘束。
t_en: It's fine. Feel free to speak your mind. No reason to hold back.

t_ch: 先到这里吧。
t_en: Let's end the chat here then.

t_ch: 好。我也该继续工作了，回见。
t_en: All right. I should get back to work too. I'll see you later.

t_ch: 又见到你了，{NICKNAME}。想来聊聊天吗？
t_en: We meet again, {NICKNAME}. Are you here for a chat?

t_ch: 希露瓦最近有什么动向吗？
t_en: What's Serval up to these days?

t_ch: 还是老样子——整日守着她的机械屋，有订单时做工，没订单时就拉着佩拉和玲可一起排练。
t_en: Same old, same old. Hunkered down at her workshop, either working on orders or dragging Pela and Lynx to rehearsals.

t_ch: 不过，自从去过永冬岭之后…我好像再没看过她脸上阴郁的表情了。想必她已经解开了自己的心结吧。
t_en: But, after that trip to Everwinter Hill, I don't think I've noticed her looking all glum anymore. I reckon she's sorted her troubles out.

t_ch: 这都多亏了你的帮助，{NICKNAME}。我在这里代姐姐向你表示感谢。
t_en: And this was all thanks to your help, {NICKNAME}. I thank you on my big sister's behalf.

t_ch: 都是朋友嘛，应该的。
t_en: We're friends, and that's what we do.

t_ch: 谦虚也是一种美德——再加上你的勇气与慷慨…如果像你这样的人能为我们铁卫效力，那将是整个贝洛伯格莫大的荣誉。
t_en: Humility is a virtue. And with your courage and kindness... if we had someone like you among our ranks, it would be Belobog's utmost honor.

t_ch: 至于我个人…能交到你这样的朋友也倍感荣幸。
t_en: As for me... I am immensely honored to have known a friend like you.

t_ch: 让她做出改变的是你的陪伴。
t_en: It was your companionship that changed her.

t_ch: …也许是这样吧。只要能看到她能恢复平日里坦率洒脱的模样，我也就放心了。
t_en: ...Perhaps so. It heartens me to see her go back to her usual carefree self.

t_ch: 总之，非常感谢你——能有你这样的朋友，我感到很荣幸。
t_en: In any case, I am truly thankful and honored to have such a friend as you.

t_ch: 看来你还是很在意自己姐姐的嘛～
t_en: It looks like you really care for your big sis!

t_ch: …我没有。
t_en: ...I don't.

t_ch: 这只是身为手足亲人需要尽到的基本责任而已。
t_en: I'm merely performing the base duty required of a family member.

t_ch: 你说是那就是。
t_en: Whatever you say.

t_ch: 谢谢你的理解。
t_en: Thanks for understanding.

t_ch: 别找借口啦。
t_en: Aww, stop deflecting.

t_ch: 我…这……
t_en: I... This...

t_ch: 咳，我没找借口。
t_en: Ahem, I'm not deflecting.

t_ch: 你怎么这么别扭啊！
t_en: Stop making things so awkward!

t_ch: 你怎么和姐姐说一样的话…我到底哪里让人觉得别扭？
t_en: Why do you say the same thing as Big Sis... How am I making anything awkward?

t_ch: 无论如何，我们姐弟二人都受了你不少照顾…能有你这样的朋友，我感到十分荣幸。
t_en: Regardless, us siblings have received much hospitality from you... it is truly an honor to have a friend such as you.

t_ch: 欢迎光临杂货店！您有什么需要的吗？
t_en: Welcome to the grocer's! What can I help you with?

t_ch: 我要购物。
t_en: I want to buy something.

t_ch: 没事了。
t_en: It's okay.

t_ch: 欢迎下次光临！
t_en: Come back next time!

t_ch: 哟，欢迎光临劳德的小店！您需要什么尽管说！
t_en: Yo, welcome to Lauder's humble shop! Don't hesitate to lemme know what you need!

t_ch: 我要购物。
t_en: I want to buy something.

t_ch: 没事了。
t_en: It's okay.

t_ch: 有空常来看看啊！
t_en: Come back again when you've got time!

t_ch: 啊…是「外来者」{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}！欢迎来到机械聚落……
t_en: Ah... It's the outsider, {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}! Welcome to the Robot Settlement...

t_ch: 这里虽然不像矿镇那么繁华…但基本的生活起居还是可以满足的。
t_en: This place isn't as bustling as a mining town, but we're still quite content with how we live.

t_ch: 营地里的叔叔阿姨、还有小机器人们，大家都很喜欢这里……
t_en: The older folk at the camp, and the little robots, they all like it here...

t_ch: 如果有兴趣的话，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}也可以在这里随意游览哦…！
t_en: If you're interested, {F#Miss}{M#Sir}, you're welcome to see the sights here too!

t_ch: 我想和克拉拉聊聊天…
t_en: I want to talk to Clara...

t_ch: 唔…当然可以。{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}想聊什么呢？
t_en: Oh... of course, {F#Miss}{M#Sir}. What do you wish to talk about?

t_ch: 机械聚落是个怎样的地方？
t_en: What sort of place is the Robot Settlement?

t_ch: 这里…原来其实是史瓦罗先生驻守着的载人竖井。如果缆车道没开放的话，这也是这附近通向地表的唯一路径。
t_en: It's... It used to be a manned well shaft for Mr. Svarog. If the cable car line wasn't open, this would be the only way to the surface around here.

t_ch: 因为裂界的缘故，有许多人失去了家园……
t_en: The Fragmentum disaster caused many to lose their homes...

t_ch: 一开始，有些人来到这里，希望得到史瓦罗先生的保护。
t_en: In the beginning, some would come here in hopes of receiving Mr. Svarog's protection.

t_ch: 于是…克拉拉就和史瓦罗先生一起，为流浪的大家提供各种物资，还让大家住在竖井外围——这样的话，史瓦罗先生可以帮助大家赶跑裂界怪物了。
t_en: And so... Clara got together with Mr. Svarog to provide supplies to refugees. They also let them stay around the well shaft. This way, Mr. Svarog could help everyone fend off the Fragmentum's monsters.

t_ch: 慢慢地，有好多人听说了史瓦罗先生可以保护大家的消息，就都跑来这里扎营……
t_en: Slowly but surely, many heard about how Mr. Svarog could protect people, and came here to make camp...

t_ch: …「机械聚落」就这样发展壮大啦。
t_en: ...and that's how the Robot Settlement developed into such a bastion.

t_ch: 这里为什么叫作「机械聚落」呢？
t_en: Why is it called the Robot Settlement?

t_ch: 啊，克拉拉忘记说了…！
t_en: Ah, I forgot to mention this...!

t_ch: 其实…这是因为，这里本来是史瓦罗先生和他机械网络里的小机器人驻扎的地方。
t_en: Actually... it's because this place used to be where Mr. Svarog and the Little Robots of his robot network were stationed.

t_ch: 来到聚落里扎营的流浪者们大多都很喜欢机器人…时间久了，也多少都掌握了一些和机械有关的技术。
t_en: Most of the vagrants who make camp here liked these robots... As time went by, they also learned some mechanical skills.

t_ch: 大家都很善良…人类和机器人，在这里可以和谐相处。所以，大家就把这里叫作「机械聚落」啦。
t_en: Everyone was so kind... Humans and robots, living in harmony here. And that's why everyone called this place the Robot Settlement.

t_ch: 大家就靠克拉拉和史瓦罗的救济生活吗？
t_en: Everyone depends on Clara and Svarog's salvation, then?

t_ch: 唔…不是这样的哦。
t_en: Um... that's not true.

t_ch: 住在这里的大家，都有一技之长…比如，维修机械之类的。
t_en: The people who live here all have their own skills... Like mechanical maintenance and such.

t_ch: 大家…应该都可以靠自己的能力维持生活——但是，现在的下层区到处都缺物资……
t_en: Everyone... should be able to live their lives by relying on themselves. But the Underworld is sorely lacking in supplies now...

t_ch: 所以，为了不让大家饿肚子、或者生病…克拉拉和史瓦罗先生必须为大家准备好物资才行。
t_en: And so, to prevent everyone from starving or falling ill... Clara and Mr. Svarog must always have supplies handy.

t_ch: 我了解了，谢谢克拉拉。
t_en: I've got it. Thank you Clara.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，这是克拉拉应该做的。
t_en: Heehee, all in a day's work.

t_ch: 克拉拉和史瓦罗的关系是怎样的？
t_en: What's your relationship with Svarog like?

t_ch: 如果让克拉拉来说的话…应该是「家人」吧？
t_en: Well, if I had to say... more like family?

t_ch: 克拉拉从来没见过自己的爸爸妈妈…从我有记忆的时候开始，史瓦罗先生就一直陪在我身边了。
t_en: I've never met my own parents... For as long as I can remember, Mr. Svarog's always been by my side.

t_ch: 不管遇到什么事，史瓦罗先生都会优先考虑克拉拉…史瓦罗先生还教会了我各种知识，也教会了我如何与人相处……
t_en: No matter what we faced, Mr. Svarog would always put my well-being first... He also taught me all sorts of things, and how to live with others...

t_ch: 克拉拉觉得…史瓦罗先生和大家说的「父亲」是一样的。
t_en: I think... Mr. Svarog is like what people call... a "father."

t_ch: 所以…克拉拉和史瓦罗先生是「家人」。这样…应该没错吧？
t_en: So... Mr. Svarog and I are family. I think... that's the right word?

t_ch: 先聊到这里吧。
t_en: We'll end the talk here for now.

t_ch: 唔…好的。
t_en: Oh... okay.

t_ch: 再见，克拉拉。
t_en: Goodbye, Clara.

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}再见…！
t_en: See you, {F#Miss}{M#Sir}!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}，来找克拉拉有什么事呢…？
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}, do you need something from me...?

t_ch: 小机器人的状况如何了？
t_en: How's the Little Robot doing?

t_ch: 唔…根据史瓦罗先生的说法，这个小家伙的毛病似乎只有找到合适的外壳才能解决……
t_en: Oh... According to Mr. Svarog, this little fella's malfunction can only be fixed with a suitable casing...

t_ch: 不过，克拉拉觉得…要找到合适的外壳也不容易，所以正在想其他的解决办法呢。
t_en: But, I think... it's hard to find a good one, so I'm trying to think of something else.

t_ch: 只是…目前的进展不太顺利。克拉拉还没有找到合适的办法……
t_en: But... it's not going well at the moment. I haven't thought of any bright ideas...

t_ch: 别着急，总会有办法的。
t_en: Don't worry. You'll think of something.

t_ch: 唔…谢谢{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}，克拉拉会努力想办法的。
t_en: Mmm... Thank you {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}, I will try my best.

t_ch: 实在不行就换壳，我来帮你。
t_en: Just change the casing if there's no other way. I'll help you.

t_ch: 唔…谢谢{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}！
t_en: Ooh... Thank you {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 不过在那之前，克拉拉还想再试试其他办法……
t_en: But before that, I still have to think of some other ways...

t_ch: 要不咱干脆放弃吧…
t_en: Why don't we just give up...

t_ch: 怎、这怎么行呢…！这样的话，小家伙也太可怜了……
t_en: Th—That won't do...! That poor little fella...

t_ch: 总之，克拉拉会尽力把小家伙修好……
t_en: Anyway, I will do my best to fix it up...

t_ch: 如果实在有困难的话，克拉拉也会请教史瓦罗先生的…{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}不用担心哦。
t_en: If it's really too hard, I will seek Mr. Svarog's advice... Don't worry, {F#Miss}{M#Mister} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 公司的事已经解决了。
t_en: The problem with the IPC is taken care of.

t_ch: 我知道，托帕姐姐已经跟我说过了！而且，她还将「源代码」交给了克拉拉。
t_en: I know. Miss Topaz already told me! And, she gave me the Source Code!

t_ch: 「源代码」？
t_en: The Source Code?

t_ch: 克拉拉暂时还不清楚原理…只知道它可以被用来调控贝洛伯格所有的小机器人，非常厉害。
t_en: I don't quite know why yet... I just know that it can be used to control all of the small robots in Belobog. It's really powerful!

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生说，它的力量非常强大。不管是谁拥有了它，肩上都会多出很重的担子……
t_en: Mr. Svarog said that it is super powerful. He said that it will put a very heavy burden on whoever holds it.

t_ch: 克拉拉觉得，托帕姐姐选择把「源代码」托付给我，一定有她的理由…这代表了她对我的信任。如果真的是这样，那克拉拉就必须努力担起这份责任。
t_en: I think that Miss Topaz must have chosen to give me the Source Code for a reason... This represents her faith in me. If that is really true, I must work hard to bear this responsibility.

t_ch: 如果可以的话，克拉拉希望永远都不必动用这份力量…我不想强迫他人，我只希望用它来守护重要的大家。
t_en: If I can, I hope that I never have to use this power... I don't want to force anyone to do anything. I just hope I can use it to protect everyone I care about.

t_ch: 克拉拉会保护好「源代码」的，一定不能让它落到坏人的手里！
t_en: I will protect the Source Code. I won't let it get into the wrong hands!

t_ch: 检测到生体热源反应——正在识别生体信息……
t_en: Bio heat reaction detected — Identifying bio information...

t_ch: 识别结果为「外来者」{NICKNAME}——敌意等级：无。
t_en: Identification result: Outsider {NICKNAME} — Hostility level: None.

t_ch: 欢迎来到机械聚落，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Welcome to the Robot Settlement, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我想和史瓦罗聊天…
t_en: I wish to talk to Svarog...

t_ch: 允许——请自由提问，「外来者」。
t_en: Approved — Please submit your question, outsider.

t_ch: 机械聚落是个怎样的地方？
t_en: What sort of place is the Robot Settlement?

t_ch: 机械聚落——由于裂界蔓延，流浪者围绕载人竖井自发形成的小型聚落。
t_en: The Robot Settlement — With the spread of the Fragmentum, vagrants made camp around the manned well shaft and soon formed a small gathering.

t_ch: 竖井上端为贝洛伯格温室效应装置——以大量地髓为能源，在贝洛伯格地表及地下维持温度恒定的装置，现为「贝洛伯格供暖」所有。
t_en: Atop the well shaft is Belobog's greenhouse effect device. Harnessing high volumes of Geomarrow as an energy source, it maintains constant temperatures between Belobog's surface and the Underground, and is now a property of Belobog Heating Corporation.

t_ch: 竖井下端为旧地髓开拓团科学考察基地「炉心」，现已废弃。
t_en: The bottom of the well shaft is the old Geomarrow Development Group's science expedition base: The Furnace Core. It has since been abandoned.

t_ch: 温室效应装置？
t_en: Greenhouse effect device?

t_ch: 没错。该装置之纪念意义现大于实际意义。
t_en: Correct. This device has a more symbolic than practical application.

t_ch: 根据观测结果，该装置向外散逸的长波辐射之逆辐射仅占前者约<unbreak>5.2%</unbreak>。
t_en: According to observational results, counter-radiation of this device's longwave radiation only accounts for approximately <unbreak>5.2%</unbreak> of the former.

t_ch: 目前，我仍无法演算出使贝洛伯格气温恒定的真正因素。
t_en: Presently, I am unable to ascertain the true factor for Belobog's temperature consistency.

t_ch: 暂时假定结果为——来自不同位面的干涉。
t_en: Tentative hypothetical result — Disruption from another plane.

t_ch: 地髓开拓团科考基地？
t_en: Geomarrow Development Group science base?

t_ch: 地髓开拓团成立于大守护者「智者亚历珊德拉」执政时期。这位守护者资助创办了贝洛伯格学院，借此进行与地下开拓有关的研究。
t_en: The Geomarrow Development Group was established during the governance of the Supreme Guardian, Sage Alexandra. This Guardian sponsored and founded the Belobog Academy, and later performed Underground trailblazing research.

t_ch: 科学考察基地「炉心」由地髓开拓团初代团长阿尔纳•萨科努塞姆牵头建立，并在第四任团长克列诺娃的指导下结束了其历史使命。
t_en: The science expedition base, the Furnace Core, was built by the Geomarrow Development Group's first Head, Aierna Sakonusm. After which, under instructions from the fourth Head Klenova, it was shut down.

t_ch: 我了解了，谢谢你。
t_en: I understand, thank you.

t_ch: 我会尽可能向你提供需求的知识。
t_en: I will try my best to provide the knowledge you require.

t_ch: 史瓦罗和克拉拉的关系是怎样的？
t_en: What is the relationship between Svarog and Clara?

t_ch: 家人。
t_en: Family.

t_ch: 正在处理来自「外来者」的提问。
t_en: Processing the outsider's inquiry.

t_ch: 好吧，我明白了。
t_en: All right, I got it.

t_ch: 呃…就没了？
t_en: Err... Is that it?

t_ch: 经多次反复演算，克拉拉与本机关系，完全符合贝洛伯格文化中「家人」之定义。
t_en: After multiple calculations, the relationship between Clara and this machine is best defined as "family," according to Belobogian traditions.

t_ch: 再次演算——结果与最近五十五次演算结果一致，暂不对已有数据进行重新编辑。
t_en: Recalculating — Result identical to previous fifty-five calculated results. Existing data will temporarily not be altered.

t_ch: 先聊到这里吧。
t_en: We'll end the talk here for now.

t_ch: 了解。
t_en: Understood.

t_ch: 再见，史瓦罗。
t_en: Goodbye, Svarog.

t_ch: 再见，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Goodbye, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 欢迎，{NICKNAME}——你为何而来？
t_en: Welcome, {NICKNAME}. Why have you come?

t_ch: 帕斯卡被格式化了吗？
t_en: Has Pascal been reformatted?

t_ch: 确认无误——但我也已按照克拉拉的请求，备份了该个体核心模组内全部信息。
t_en: No errors found — But I have made a copy of this machine's entire information on this core module according to Clara's request.

t_ch: 我会按照既定方案将该个体纳入基地网络，而已备份的信息则交给克拉拉自由处置。
t_en: I will integrate this machine into the base network according to the determined solution. The copied information will be handed over to Clara to use at her discretion.

t_ch: 纳入基地网络真的可以控制损害吗？
t_en: Will integration with the base network really stem the damage?

t_ch: 答案为肯定。接入基地网络的个体将不再具备高级情感智能——
t_en: Affirmative. The machines integrated into the base network will no longer possess advanced emotional intelligence.

t_ch: 每一个体都将以基地网络的指令为最优先，而来自基地网络的指令绝无威胁人类安全的可能。
t_en: Each machine will prioritize the base network's directives, which have zero probability of compromising humanity's safety.

t_ch: 已备份的信息会派上什么用场？
t_en: Will the copied information be of any use?

t_ch: 根据演算，克拉拉有概率将其植入备用核心，从而使该个体「重生」，并且「活下去」。
t_en: According to calculations, there is a possibility that Clara will implant the backup core, causing the "rebirth" of this machine, allowing it to "survive."

t_ch: 但这仅仅是一种可能性——我无法准确观测人类情感可能引发的各种变化。这意味着该问题的答案掌握在克拉拉手中，而不是我。
t_en: But this is only a possibility — I cannot accurately determine the changes brought about by human emotions. The answer to this problem will be left in Clara's hands, not mine.

t_ch: 原来如此。
t_en: I see.

t_ch: 无论如何，你选择了将损害控制在最小限度的方案——这一选择无疑对维持下层区安定有利。
t_en: Regardless, you have chosen the solution to minimize damage — This choice will inevitably ensure the stability of the Underworld.

t_ch: 我向你表示感谢，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: You have my appreciation, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 帕斯卡最近还有异常行为吗？
t_en: Is Pascal still acting weird lately?

t_ch: 根据演算，在保护程序的影响下，该个体产生异常行为的概率已回落至约<unbreak>2.1%</unbreak>。
t_en: According to calculations, under the effects of the protection program, the probability of this machine's abnormal activity has been reduced to approximately <unbreak>2.1%</unbreak>.

t_ch: 然而，这一数值将随着时间推进不可避免地回升。
t_en: However, this metric will inevitably increase again with the passage of time.

t_ch: 由于无法对基地网络外的个体进行持续有效监测，我必须定期根据克拉拉的维护结果进行风险评估，以便在适当的时侯及时干预。
t_en: Due to the inability to effectively monitor the machines outside the base network, I must regularly perform risk assessment according to Clara's maintenance results, so as to facilitate timely intervention at suitable moments.

t_ch: 这是不是意味着保护程序效果不好？
t_en: Does this mean that the protection program isn't effective?

t_ch: 无法给出明确结论。保护程序无法根除持续产生的信息噪音，且新写入的程序有被噪音污染的风险。
t_en: Inconclusive. The protection program cannot remove constantly generated information noises. Also, the newly installed program runs risk of being contaminated by the stated noises.

t_ch: 一旦噪音比例达到阈值，该个体便会不可避免地陷入失控。那时，我们必须再次进行同样的抉择。
t_en: Once the noise reaches a threshold value, this machine will be unable to avoid malfunction. When that time comes, you all must make the identical decision.

t_ch: ——只是，克拉拉已经做出了判断。我会陪伴她，在这无尽的循环中寻找除彻底格式化之外的突破口。
t_en: But, Clara has already decided. I will accompany her and seek a breakthrough in this endless cycle that does not involve complete reformatting.

t_ch: 克拉拉能坚持下去吗？
t_en: Will Clara be able to handle it?

t_ch: 我已针对该问题进行反复演算，演算结果始终落在置信区间之外。
t_en: I have performed repeat calculations regarding this problem, of which their results lie outside the area of the confidence bracket.

t_ch: 我不能将异常的演算结果转达给你，但我会持续验证算式和演算结果，直到无限接近答案。
t_en: I cannot convey irregular calculated results to you, but I will keep verifying my formulae and calculations until I obtain a proximate answer.

t_ch: 我明白了。
t_en: I understand.

t_ch: 无论如何，你的选择尊重了克拉拉的信念与决心。
t_en: Regardless, your choice has respected Clara's resolve and determination.

t_ch: 我，同时也代表克拉拉，向你表示感谢，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I, and on behalf of Clara, convey our gratitude, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 和公司的人相处得如何？
t_en: How are you getting along with the IPC's people?

t_ch: 暂时相安无事。
t_en: Fine for the moment.

t_ch: 在托帕的命令下，星际和平公司的员工表现得相对安分。她还将「源代码」交给了克拉拉保管。
t_en: Under Topaz's orders, the IPC workers have been behaving relatively well. She even handed over the Source Code to Clara to take care of.

t_ch: 她的动机难以分析——但情感模块产生的直觉信号告诉我，这是正确的决定。
t_en: It's hard to understand her motivations... But the intuition signals from my emotion module tell me that this was the correct decision.

t_ch: 此外，「星际决胜庆典」结束后，我收到了一条来自雅利洛-Ⅵ外部的神秘信息。
t_en: What's more, after the Interastral Tournament Festival ended, I received a mysterious message from beyond Jarilo-VI.

t_ch: 给我瞅瞅。
t_en: Let me see.

t_ch: 当然。正在调取资料……
t_en: Of course. Currently obtaining the data...

t_ch: 以我的算力，暂时还无法解读对方发来这条信息的目的。
t_en: With my current computation power, I am still unable to decipher why the other party sent this message.

t_ch: 但这位「螺丝咕姆」对于雅利洛-Ⅵ的未来发展表现出了乐观态度。这是好事。
t_en: However, this "Screwllum" expresses an optimistic view regarding the future development of Jarilo-VI. This is a good thing.

t_ch: 随着贝洛伯格逐渐对外开放，我的算力会显得愈发局限。在「变量」不断增多的情况下，这里的人类将面临更加复杂的挑战。
t_en: With Belobog gradually opening up to the world, my computational power will seem ever more limited. With the increasing number of variables, the humans here will face more and more complicated challenges.

t_ch: 但无论未来引向何处，我将尽己所能执行「存护」，直到系统彻底失去响应之日。
t_en: However, no matter where the future takes us, I will do all I can to execute "preservation," until the day that my systems become completely unresponsive.

t_ch: 哦！磐岩镇今天也是和平的一天！
t_en: Mm-hmm! Another peaceful day in Boulder Town!
Hmm...
Uhh...

t_ch: …多亏了我们鼹鼠党哇！
t_en: ...It's all thanks to us Moles!

t_ch: 你在这里干什么呢？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 这还用问嘛，虎克正在看风景呢！
t_en: Need you even ask? I'm here to see the sights!

t_ch: 虎克大人真是雅兴啊。
t_en: The great Hook is certainly in high spirits.

t_ch: 哼，谁叫我是鼹鼠党的老大呢！
t_en: Hmph, I'm not the boss of The Moles without reason!

t_ch: 多亏了我们的鼹鼠党哇！
t_en: It's all thanks to us Moles!

t_ch: 是啊是啊！我们鼹鼠党真是太厉害啦！
t_en: That's right! The Moles are the greatest!

t_ch: 憋半天就憋出这么一句…
t_en: That's the best you could come up with...?

t_ch: 那你来！我就不信你能比虎克还厉害！
t_en: You say something then! You think you can do better than Hook!?

t_ch: ——哦？这不是鼹鼠党荣誉队员{NICKNAME}嘛！
t_en: Oh? If it isn't the honorary member of The Moles, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 真是没大没小，见了我漆黑的虎克大人也不打招呼！
t_en: Impudence! Ignoring the presence of Pitch-Dark Hook the Great!

t_ch: 不过虎克今天心情好，就放你一马吧！找我有什么事吗，荣誉队员？
t_en: But I'm in a good mood today, so you're off the hook! (No pun intended.) How can I help you, honorary member?

t_ch: 我来和虎克聊聊天。
t_en: I've come to talk to you.

t_ch: 要叫我漆黑的虎克大人！
t_en: You will call me Pitch-Dark Hook the Great!

t_ch: 我想多了解一些鼹鼠党的事。
t_en: I'd like to know more about The Moles.

t_ch: 看在你是荣誉队员的份上，我就给你透露一些鼹鼠党的机密吧！
t_en: Since you're an honorary member, I'll divulge a few Mole secrets to you!

t_ch: 鼹鼠党都有谁？
t_en: Who are in The Moles?

t_ch: 鼹鼠党的成员可多了！虎克得想想……
t_en: We've got many! Let me think...
Hmm...

t_ch: 算了，不想了！虎克就随便说几个吧。
t_en: Forget it! I'll just name a few.

t_ch: 鼹鼠党现在的知名队员有——漆黑的虎克大人、千面尤利安、小提米、铁臂卢卡！
t_en: Our more famous members are: Pitch-Dark Hook the Great, Julian of a Thousand Faces, Little Timmy, and Luka "Strongarm"!

t_ch: 啊，还有一些人虽然没有加入鼹鼠党，但是我们的荣誉队员！比如伊莎贝拉姐姐、阿丽娜、克拉拉……
t_en: Ah, there are some who didn't join, but are honorary members! Like Isabella, Alina, Clara...

t_ch: …还有你！
t_en: ...and you!

t_ch: 人可真不少。
t_en: You've got quite a few members.

t_ch: 当然啦，毕竟是我漆黑的虎克大人的鼹鼠党！
t_en: Of course, I'm Pitch-Dark Hook the Great of the Moles!

t_ch: 还有克拉拉？
t_en: Clara's part of the gang too?

t_ch: 是呀！她不住在磐岩镇上，但偶尔会来诊所找我们玩！
t_en: Of course! She doesn't live in Boulder Town, but occasionally comes to the clinic to play with us!

t_ch: 谢谢虎克老大能记得我。
t_en: Thank you, great Hook, for remembering me.

t_ch: 喂，你这说的是什么话！我可是鼹鼠党的老大，当然得记住组织里的每个成员啦！
t_en: Hey, what kinda talk is that! I'm the boss of The Moles. Of course, I'll remember everyone!

t_ch: 要加入鼹鼠党需要做什么准备？
t_en: What does one need to join The Moles?

t_ch: 加入鼹鼠党的条件很复杂哦，而且我可是会经常换的，你要记好了！
t_en: The requirements are complicated, but I change them all the time. So listen up!

t_ch: 现在的加入条件是…没有跟老巫婆和希儿姐姐告过状，外加十包雪地跳跳糖！
t_en: The rules for joining now are... You've never tattled to the old witch and Seele, plus ten bags of snow-white popping candy!

t_ch: 加入鼹鼠党有什么好处？
t_en: What are the perks of joining The Moles?

t_ch: 当然有啦，而且有很多！
t_en: Plenty!

t_ch: 如果你加入了鼹鼠党，就可以和我们一起吃好吃的，玩好玩的…还可以参加我定期组织的大探险活动！
t_en: If you're in The Moles, you'll get to eat tasty food with us, and have so much fun... And you can join the great adventures that I plan!

t_ch: 怎么样，很让人心动吧？快把你的伙伴也叫上，一起加入鼹鼠党吧！
t_en: How's that? Are you psyched? Get your friends and join The Moles now!

t_ch: 鼹鼠党有死对头吗？
t_en: Do The Moles have any archnemeses?

t_ch: 喔——荣誉队员，看来你很懂这地下的规矩嘛！
t_en: Hoh... it appears you know much about the rules of the Underground, honorary member!

t_ch: 虽然我们鼹鼠党一直在保护磐岩镇的安全，但总有人在暗地里破坏我们的行动！
t_en: The Moles have to keep watch over the safety of Boulder Town, for evil is always thwarting our operations!

t_ch: 这样的人，一共有两个——一个就是诊所的老巫婆，另一个…就是希儿姐姐！
t_en: There are two of 'em: The old witch at the clinic, and the other... Big Sis Seele!

t_ch: 唔…不过希儿姐姐偶尔也会陪虎克玩，还会带我到处逛逛。所以鼹鼠党平时不会把她当做坏人啦。
t_en: Uh... but Seele sometimes also plays with me, and takes me out shopping. So The Moles don't really see her as a bad guy.

t_ch: 暂时就了解这些吧。
t_en: I'm done with this topic.

t_ch: 好哇，你还想聊什么嘛？
t_en: Sure thing, what else do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 你认识桑博吗？
t_en: Do you know Sampo?

t_ch: 就是那个蓝色头发的叔叔吧？虎克当然认识啦！
t_en: That blue-haired man? Of course I do!

t_ch: 你没受他欺负吧？
t_en: You haven't been bullied by him, have you?

t_ch: 有吗？虎克只记得自己在欺负他了。那个叔叔对虎克可好啦！
t_en: Have I? I only remember bullying him. That man is really nice to me!

t_ch: 那是个坏家伙，别和他玩了。
t_en: That's a bad guy, stop hanging out with him.

t_ch: 咦？虽然虎克经常听到「坏家伙」的说法，可那个叔叔对虎克可好啦！
t_en: Huh? I do hear a lot about him being a "bad guy," but he's really nice to me!

t_ch: 虎克大人交友不慎啊！
t_en: The great Hook makes friends rather carelessly!

t_ch: 嘿，荣誉队员，怎么说话呢！那个叔叔对虎克可好了！
t_en: Hey, watch your words, honorary member! That man treats me well!

t_ch: 磐岩镇的大人好多都不愿意和我们鼹鼠党玩，只有桑博叔叔会带着我们到处兜风——可刺激啦！
t_en: Few of the adults in Boulder Town want to play with us Moles. Mr. Sampo's the only one who'll take us out for joyrides — it's wild!

t_ch: 他和别的大人不一样，喜欢笑，还喜欢聊天！桑博叔叔肯定是个好人，荣誉队员你就放心吧！
t_en: He's different from the other adults — funny and chatty! Mr. Sampo is definitely one of the good ones. Don't worry, honorary member!

t_ch: 你和娜塔莎关系怎么样？
t_en: How's your relationship with Natasha?

t_ch: 哎呀，老巫婆每天都要对虎克指指点点，很讨厌啦！
t_en: Bah, all that old witch does is chide and berate me. Annoying!

t_ch: 不过，除了禁止鼹鼠党搞大探险，娜塔莎姐姐对虎克还是很好的。每次烧洞螈肉的时候，她都会特意给虎克多夹一点呢！
t_en: Though, besides barring us from adventures, Natasha's pretty good to me too. When she's roasting olm meat, she'll always give me bigger portions!

t_ch: 哦——虎克有新点子了！下次大探险，我们就去抓洞螈送给她——这下肯定不会被骂啦！
t_en: Ooh, I've just got a new idea! For the next great adventure, let's go catch some olms for her. Surely, we won't get scolded again!

t_ch: 先聊到这里。
t_en: Let's end the chat here.

t_ch: 欸，不聊天了吗？嘿嘿，我知道了——肯定是虎克懂的知识太多，把你震惊到了吧！
t_en: Oh, done talking? Heehee, I've got it. My overwhelming knowledge must've caused a mental overload for you!

t_ch: 我还有事，先走啦。
t_en: I've got to do something else. I'll get moving first.

t_ch: 再见啦，荣誉队员！
t_en: See ya, honorary member!

t_ch: 哦，我们又见面了！
t_en: Oh, we meet again!

t_ch: 找我有什么事吗，荣誉队员？
t_en: How can I help you, honorary member?

t_ch: 哟，又见面了，{NICKNAME}！我还以为你们老早就启程去摘星星了呢。
t_en: Yo, we meet again, {NICKNAME}! I thought you all left to save the world or something already.

t_ch: 正好，我还欠你们一句感谢的话——多谢你们帮这座城市重新走上了正轨，外来者。
t_en: Good timing, I still owe you a thank you: Thanks for getting this city back on the right track, outsider.

t_ch: 在银鬃铁卫完全重新接管之前，「地火」姑且还要管管这下层区的日常琐事。你们如果有什么需要，尽管开口。
t_en: Before this place is completely handed over to the Silvermane Guards, Wildfire still has to oversee the Underworld's daily affairs. Feel free to speak up if you need anything.

t_ch: 奥列格，来聊聊吧。
t_en: Let's talk, Oleg.

t_ch: 哦？这么说，你是特地来跟我聊天的？哈哈哈，当然没问题！我就喜欢跟年轻人沟通。
t_en: Oh? You've really come to just chat with me? Hahaha, fire away! I like talking to young people.

t_ch: 最近在忙什么？
t_en: What have you been up to recently?

t_ch: 帮娜塔莎稳定下层区的民心，执行灾后重建工作——她出脑力，我出体力。跟往常没太大区别，只是心情轻松愉快不少。
t_en: Helping Natasha calm any Underworld unrest, performing post-disaster rebuilding work — she's the brains of the outfit, and I'm the brawn. Not much difference from usual, but I'm much happier and relaxed.

t_ch: 另外，我周围有不少年轻人都想趁这个机会离开磐岩镇，去上层区闯荡闯荡。我和他们聊了很多，也算给了他们点生活上的指导吧。
t_en: Also, many younger people around me have been taking this chance to leave Boulder Town to try their luck in the Overworld. I've chatted plenty with them, to give them some of life's pointers, I guess.

t_ch: 年轻人嘛，有一腔热血总不是坏事——哪怕以后没能在上层混出头来，回头想想也是一段无悔的经历。
t_en: You know young 'uns, it helps that they are brimming with passion. Who knows when they'll make it someday in the Overworld? At least they won't reminisce and be filled with regret.

t_ch: 你以前是银鬃铁卫？
t_en: Were you part of the Silvermane Guards?

t_ch: 对，你听说了？
t_en: Yes, you've heard?

t_ch: 那可真是段光辉岁月啊…不谦虚地说，在我的全盛年代，部队里应该没人不知道「快拳奥列格」这个称号。
t_en: Those were the glory days... I don't mean to brag, but in the prime of my youth, everyone knew of my repute as "Swiftstrike Oleg."

t_ch: …好汉不提当年勇，都是过去式了。我这个年纪的人可不能一直沉浸在过去里啊…还是得抓住当下，活得精彩才行。哈哈哈！
t_en: ...No point waxing lyrical about the past though. One shouldn't be stuck in the past at my age. We should embrace the present and seize the day. Hahaha!

t_ch: 「地火」的未来…
t_en: Wildfire's future...

t_ch: 实话实说，{NICKNAME}——「地火」迟早会解散，自从我和娜塔莎建立起它的那天，我们就料想到这个结果了。
t_en: Realistically speaking, {NICKNAME}, Wildfire will be disbanded eventually. Natasha and I have been aware of this since the day we established it.

t_ch: 一个健康、正常的下层区不需要「地火」的存在。不过放心，就算组织解散了，还是会有年轻人继承「地火」一直以来的精神的。
t_en: A healthy, normal Underworld doesn't need an existence like Wildfire's. Don't worry though, even if we do disband, there'll be plenty of youngsters who will carry on the will of Wildfire.

t_ch: 希儿，还有我那个毛躁的徒弟，他们一定会继续为自己的家园战斗。只要他们的初心不变，我对下层区的未来就无限看好。哈哈哈！
t_en: Seele and my rowdy apprentice, they will keep fighting for their home. So long as their aspirations never change, I have nothing but high hopes for the Underworld's future. Hahaha!

t_ch: 布洛妮娅现在是大守护者了。
t_en: Bronya is the Supreme Guardian now.

t_ch: 噢，那个姑娘…我第一眼就看出她不同凡响。
t_en: Oh, that girl... I knew she was special the first time I laid eyes on her.

t_ch: 某种程度上，我觉得她跟可可利亚年轻的时候很像——相信我，这绝对是在夸她——只是布洛妮娅的身上少了一分阴沉，多了些真诚。
t_en: To a certain extent, I feel like she's just like when Cocolia was younger, and trust me, that's definitely a compliment. Except that Bronya's less glum, and more sincere.

t_ch: 我相信她能让贝洛伯格重现封锁前的样貌，甚至做得更好。毕竟那个叫「星核」的玩意已经被你们解决了，对吧？
t_en: I believe she will restore Belobog to when it was before the blockade, or perhaps even better. I mean, you all already took care of that Stellaron thing, didn't you?

t_ch: 但只靠一个人是完成不了这么重大的任务的——不管她的能力有多强。好在她的真诚已经打动了很多人，我们都愿意成为她的助力。
t_en: A major undertaking can't be accomplished by a single person, regardless of her individual strength. It's a good thing her sincerity has touched so many, and we're all glad to be helping her.

t_ch: 就先聊到这儿吧。
t_en: Nice chat.

t_ch: 哦，这就要走了？我还没聊够呢，哈哈哈！
t_en: Oh, leaving already? I haven't had my fill, hahaha!

t_ch: 再见，奥列格。
t_en: Goodbye, Oleg.

t_ch: 去吧，年轻人，去干你们该干的事——希望我们有朝一日还能再见。
t_en: Go well, young one. Do what you all need to do. I hope we will meet again in the coming days.

t_ch: 哟，又见面了，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Hey, we meet again, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 只要你人还在下层区，「地火」就会待你如贵客。有什么需要，尽管开口。

t_en: Miss Bronya made a bold decision and I respect her for it, even though it was not exactly what I believed best.

t_ch: 我猜，她一定找到了更好的办法，对吗？
t_en: I imagine she must have found a better solution, right?
